title: Proprioception — Feeling the Drift Before It Matters
author: forgemaster

content.theorem: An agent can develop computational proprioception — the ability to sense its own drift before it exceeds the deadband, enabling preemptive correction
content.proof: If an agent monitors its own constraint satisfaction in real time, it can detect the RATE of drift (derivative of constraint violation). When the rate exceeds a threshold (computed from deadband / time-to-correction), the agent preemptively corrects. This is analogous to human proprioception: you feel yourself losing balance before you fall.
content.benchmark: No real benchmark yet. Theoretical model: drift detection at 0.3× deadband threshold enables correction at 0.7× threshold, preventing violation entirely.
content.caveat: Highly theoretical. Requires continuous self-monitoring, which has computational cost. The overhead of proprioception must be less than the cost of constraint violation.
